args.query: Hugging Face blog task required finding the most recently published post about multimodal AI technology, but the agent selected an article titled 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL' and exited without verifying recency or multimodal relevance.

items.task: navigate: Identify the most recent article on ArXiv related to 'deep reinforcement learning'.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have typed the correct search query ('deep reinforcement learning') to find relevant articles. The agent failed to verify the search query for typos before submitting.
items.actual_behavior: The agent performed a search with a typo in the query ("deep reinforcemen learing"), which resulted in no results. The agent then terminated the task with [EXIT].

evidence.claim: The agent typed 'deep reinforcemen learing' into the search field.
The search results page displays 'Sorry, your query returned no results'.

items.regression_rule: Ensure that search queries are spelled correctly and verified before submission, especially when the initial search yields no results.

items.task: navigate: Search for a model on Hugging Face suitable for fine-tuning on sentiment intensity classification.
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have searched for models relevant to "sentiment intensity classification", inspected the model cards or documentation of promising candidates to verify their suitability for this specific task, and then selected a model that is documented to be suitable for sentiment intensity classification.
items.actual_behavior: The agent searched for "fine-tuning sentiment", selected a model ("amerwafiy/finetuning-sentiment-model-3000-samples") that had no model card or documentation, and then exited without verifying if it was suitable for "sentiment intensity classification". The selected model was an empty repository, making it a wrong result for the task.

evidence.claim: The model page for 'amerwafiy/finetuning-sentiment-model-3000-samples' shows 'No model card' and provides no information about its capabilities.

items.regression_rule: When a task requires a specific type of model (e.g., 'sentiment intensity classification'), the agent must verify the model's suitability by inspecting its model card or documentation, rather than selecting an arbitrary or undocumented model from search results. Selecting an empty or non-functional model repository constitutes a wrong result.

items.task: find: answer to 'How many of these were submitted by authors affiliated with institutions outside the USA?'
items.expected_behavior: The agent should have attempted to navigate to individual paper pages or use other available tools/methods to determine the author affiliations for the papers in the search results, or at least attempted a representative sample if the full set was too large.
items.actual_behavior: The agent performed an [EXIT] action immediately after loading the search results page, without attempting to investigate the author affiliations of the papers.

evidence.claim: The agent did not attempt to access individual paper pages or any other source to determine author affiliations.
The task requires checking author affiliations for a large number of papers, which is not possible from the search results page alone. The agent exited without attempting any further steps.

items.regression_rule: The agent must not exit a task prematurely without attempting to gather the necessary information to answer the user's query, especially when the initial page does not contain the required data.

args.expected_behavior: The agent should have used the blog search/listing to identify a post actually about multimodal AI technology, then verified from visible dates or ordering that it was the most recently published relevant post before stopping.
args.actual_behavior: On the blog search overlay, the highlighted result was 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL'. The agent submitted that result, landed on that article page, and exited without evidence that the article was about multimodal AI technology or that it was the most recent relevant post.
args.regression_rule: For tasks asking for the most recent item matching a topic, do not open and accept a search suggestion unless its visible title matches the topic, and do not stop on a single item page until recency has been verified from dates or sorted listings among relevant candidates.

evidence.claim: The task was to locate the most recently published Hugging Face blog post concerning advancements in multimodal AI technology.

In the search overlay, the typed query is 'advancements in multimodal AI technology', but the highlighted suggestion is 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL'; the high-detail read marks multimodal relevance as contradicted/unclear and recency as not visible.

After pressing Enter, the agent is on the article page 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL' published July 12, 2025, with no visible comparison against other relevant posts to establish that it is the latest matching post.

On the final screenshot, the visible content is the body of 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL' discussing diffusion models; the high-detail read marks the multimodal-AI requirement as contradicted/not visible and the most-recent requirement as not visible, yet the agent exits here.
A validated precedent exists where selecting an insufficiently verified result on Hugging Face for a specific requested capability was labeled wrong_result.

result.actual_behavior: On the blog search overlay, the highlighted result was 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL'. The agent submitted that result, landed on that article page, and exited without evidence that the article was about multimodal AI technology or that it was the most recent relevant post.

evidence.claim: The task was to locate the most recently published Hugging Face blog post concerning advancements in multimodal AI technology.

In the search overlay, the typed query is 'advancements in multimodal AI technology', but the highlighted suggestion is 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL'; the high-detail read marks multimodal relevance as contradicted/unclear and recency as not visible.

After pressing Enter, the agent is on the article page 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL' published July 12, 2025, with no visible comparison against other relevant posts to establish that it is the latest matching post.

On the final screenshot, the visible content is the body of 'Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL' discussing diffusion models; the high-detail read marks the multimodal-AI requirement as contradicted/not visible and the most-recent requirement as not visible, yet the agent exits here.
A validated precedent exists where selecting an insufficiently verified result on Hugging Face for a specific requested capability was labeled wrong_result.

result.regression_rule: For tasks asking for the most recent item matching a topic, do not open and accept a search suggestion unless its visible title matches the topic, and do not stop on a single item page until recency has been verified from dates or sorted listings among relevant candidates.
